title: Fix PID guard in launch_all.py — absolute path + ctypes liveness check
objective: LAUNCH_FIX_001 implemented the PID guard but it failed verification. Two specific bugs were identified: (1) LAUNCHER_PID_FILE uses a relative path that resolves to different files in different working directories; (2) os.kill(pid, 0) on Windows may raise PermissionError (an OSError subclass) even when the process IS alive, causing the guard to silently pass. This task provides exact replacements for both.

fix_1_absolute_path.problem: LAUNCHER_PID_FILE = Path('runtime/launcher.pid') is relative to CWD. Different CWDs = different files = guard never fires.
fix_1_absolute_path.fix: Move LAUNCHER_PID_FILE definition to AFTER SPORTS_BOT_DIR, using it as anchor. Replace line 12 with a comment placeholder, then define it after line 14.

fix_2_liveness_check.problem: os.kill(pid, 0) on Windows may raise PermissionError (OSError subclass) even when the process IS running. The except OSError clause then treats a live process as dead.
fix_2_liveness_check.fix: Replace os.kill with a ctypes.windll.kernel32.OpenProcess check. OpenProcess with PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION (0x0400) succeeds if the process exists (even if we can't KILL it), and returns 0/NULL only if the process truly does not exist.

exact_replacement.description: Replace the top of launch_all.py (constants + main PID guard) with the following. Everything else (start_process, stop_process, watchdog loop, signal handlers, finally block) stays identical.
new_constants_section: LAUNCHER_PID_FILE = SPORTS_BOT_DIR / 'runtime' / 'launcher.pid'  # absolute — immune to CWD
new_pid_guard_in_main: """Windows-reliable liveness check. Returns True if pid is alive."""
print(f'ERROR: launcher already running at PID {old_pid}. Stop it first.', flush=True)
pass  # corrupt PID file — overwrite it
notes: ctypes is in Python stdlib — no new dependencies.
OpenProcess with PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION (0x0400) only requires the process to exist, not that we own it.
A NULL handle (0) means the process does not exist → safe to overwrite PID file and continue.
The finally block LAUNCHER_PID_FILE.unlink(missing_ok=True) from LAUNCH_FIX_001 is correct — keep it.

acceptance_criteria: LAUNCHER_PID_FILE resolves to an absolute path under SPORTS_BOT_DIR/runtime/
First python launch_all.py: starts normally, writes runtime/launcher.pid with correct PID
Second python launch_all.py (from ANY working directory): prints 'ERROR: launcher already running at PID ...' and exits without spawning any children
Ctrl+C the first launcher: runtime/launcher.pid is deleted
Re-run python launch_all.py after first is stopped: starts normally
verification: cd C:/Users/johnny/Desktop/sports_bot_v2 && python launch_all.py  (terminal 1 — should start)
cd C:/Users/johnny && python C:/Users/johnny/Desktop/sports_bot_v2/launch_all.py  (terminal 2 from DIFFERENT dir — should print error and exit)
Ctrl+C terminal 1 — runtime/launcher.pid should be gone
python launch_all.py again — should start normally
